name: Summer Academy for Math and Science (SAMS)

cost.notes: Fully funded: tuition, housing, meals covered. Need-based scholarships available.

prior_knowledge: Interest in engineering, CS, or math

essays.prompt: Describe a challenge you have overcome and how it shaped your approach to learning.
tips: CMU values resilience and self-awareness — show how adversity strengthened your academic commitment

essays.prompt: Why are you interested in STEM, and how would SAMS help you achieve your goals?
tips: Be specific about your STEM interests — SAMS is for students with genuine technical passion, not casual curiosity

outcomes.college_impact: SAMS alumni are strong candidates for CMU, MIT, and Ivy League programs. The program is especially valued for students from underrepresented or disadvantaged backgrounds.
outcomes.typical_results: Many alumni attend Carnegie Mellon, MIT, and other top STEM schools. Participants receive CMU credit for completed coursework.

what_they_look_for: Academic preparedness in mathematics and science
First-generation college students and underrepresented backgrounds given priority
Genuine interest in engineering, computer science, or mathematics
Potential to thrive in a rigorous college-level environment

blurbs.short: A free 6-week residential program at Carnegie Mellon University for rising seniors. Students take college-level courses in mathematics, computer science, and engineering while working on group research projects. All tuition, housing, and meals are fully funded, and participants earn CMU college credit.
blurbs.long: SAMS (Summer Academy for Math and Science) at Carnegie Mellon is one of the most prestigious fully-funded STEM pre-college programs in the US. The program enrolls approximately 90-100 rising seniors each year from a pool of over 1,000 applicants. Students live on the CMU campus and take intensive coursework in mathematics, computer science, and engineering, working alongside CMU faculty and graduate students. A distinctive feature is that courses are transcribed by CMU, giving participants genuine college credit. The program has a strong focus on community and support, particularly for students from underrepresented or disadvantaged backgrounds. SAMS alumni are frequently found at CMU, MIT, Stanford, and Ivy League institutions.

curriculum: College-level courses in math, CS, and engineering; group research projects; seminars with CMU faculty; residential community life on the CMU campus.
